title: Training Load
subtitle: CTL · ATL · Form (TSB)
tsbVeryFresh: Very fresh — possible fitness loss from undertraining
tsbRisk: Overreaching risk — reduce training load immediately
tssDesc: TSS is the building block of every metric. Each session's load = duration (min) × zone_factor². One all-out hour at threshold ≈ 100 TSS.
atlDesc: 7-day exponential moving average of daily TSS. Reacts quickly — rises after hard days, drops fast with rest. Represents how tired you are right now.
ctlDesc: 42-day exponential moving average of daily TSS. Builds slowly over months and reflects true aerobic adaptation. Losing it takes weeks, not days.
tsbDesc: TSB = CTL − ATL. When chronic fitness outweighs acute fatigue you are 'in form'. The goal is to peak with TSB between +5 and +25 on race day.
noDataDesc: Log some workouts to see your load trends.
interpTitle: What your numbers mean for you
interpSubtitle: Relative to your own baseline — not global averages
ctlTier1: Just starting — very low training base
ctlTier6: Elite level — very high chronic load
atlNoBase: No baseline yet — log more workouts
atlHigh: {{pct}}% above fitness — high load, monitor closely
summaryRed: Your fatigue is far exceeding your fitness base. This is the overreaching zone — injury and illness risk is elevated. Take 2–4 easy days before resuming hard training.
summaryOrange: You are accumulating meaningful fatigue. This is expected during a build phase but requires monitoring. Plan 1–2 recovery sessions before your next hard block.
summaryYellow: Solid training load with moderate fatigue accumulation. Your fitness base is absorbing the stress well. Prioritise sleep and nutrition to maximise adaptation.
summaryBlue: You are very fresh — good for race day but if you are not tapering, consider adding a stimulus session. Prolonged freshness can reverse fitness gains.
summaryFresh: Excellent form. Your body has absorbed recent training and you are primed for performance. Ideal window for a key workout, race simulation, or event.
summaryNormal: Normal productive training load. Your fitness and fatigue are in balance — the ideal zone for consistent adaptation. Keep the pattern, respect your rest days.
warningRed: persistent soreness, elevated resting HR, poor sleep, low motivation
warningOrange: unusual muscle soreness, heavier than normal legs, disrupted sleep
